title_en: Want This Hearing Aid? Well, Who Do You Know?
title_zh: 你想要这种助听器吗？那你认识谁？
rubric_en: AI-powered startup Fortell has become a secret handshake for the privileged hearing-impaired crowd who swear by the product. Now, it wants to be in your ears.
rubric_zh: 人工智能驱动的创业公司 Fortell 已经成为纽约上流社交圈听障人士心中的“秘密暗号”与身份象征，这些用户对它爱不释手。现在，它想进驻你的耳朵。

paragraphs.en: A secret is percolating at dinner parties, salons, and cocktail gatherings among the august New York City elite. It’s whispered in the circles of financial masters of the universe, Hollywood stars, and owners of sports teams. Have you heard about Fortell?
paragraphs.zh: 一项秘密正在纽约市名流聚会、沙龙和鸡尾酒会中悄然传开。它在金融大鳄、好莱坞明星和体育队老板的圈子里被低声谈及。你听说过 Fortell 吗？

paragraphs.en: Many haven’t—or if they did hear, they might not have made out the words through noisy cross-conversations. Once they do know—particularly if they’re boomers—they want it desperately. Fortell is a hearing aid, one that claims to use AI to provide a dramatically superior aural experience. The chosen few included in its beta test claim that it seems to top the performance of high-end devices they’d been unhappily using.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人还没听说——或者即使听到了，也可能在热闹的交谈声中没听清内容。一旦他们听明白了，尤其是婴儿潮一代的人，便会几乎迫不及待地想要入手。Fortell 是一种助听器，声称借助人工智能带来大幅优于传统产品的听觉体验。参与内测的“少数入选者”表示，它的表现似乎超越了他们过去使用却并不满意的高端设备。

paragraphs.en: These testers have made pilgrimages to Fortell’s headquarters on the fifth floor of a WeWork facility in New York City’s trendy SoHo neighborhood, where they were fitted for the hearing aids—which from the outside look pretty much like standard, over-the-ear, teardrop-shaped devices. But the big moment comes when a Fortell staffer takes them down to street level. There, among street clatter, honking cabs, and delivery trucks backing up to luxury stores, they are asked to conduct a conversation with a Fortell worker. Two other employees stand behind them, adding their own loud discourse to the urban cacophony.
paragraphs.zh: 这些体验者曾多次专程前往纽约时尚的苏活区 WeWork 办公楼五楼的 Fortell 总部，在那里为自己配戴助听器——从外观上看，它几乎和常见的耳戴式、泪滴形设备一样。真正关键的一幕发生在一位 Fortell 工作人员把他们带到街头之后。就在街道喧嚣、出租车喇叭、送货卡车倒车前往奢侈店的环境中，他们被要求与一名 Fortell 员工进行对话。另有两名员工站在他们身后，将自己的大声交谈也加入这场城市噪杂中。

paragraphs.en: Despite the din, the testers clearly make out what the person in front of them is saying. The clouds lift. Angels croon. “This was so incredible that I burst into tears,” says Ashley Tudor, one of the seemingly few beta testers who isn’t famous or powerful (though she is married to a venture capitalist).
paragraphs.zh: 尽管环境嘈杂，体验者们仍清晰听出了站在他们前面那个人在说什么。乌云散去。天籁般的轻声仿佛响起。“这一刻太不可思议了，我当场就哭了，”一位看起来并不出名也不权势的内测用户 Ashley Tudor 说，尽管她的丈夫是一位风险投资家。

paragraphs.en: Among the age-related-hearing-loss set, getting into the Fortell beta test has become a weird status symbol, the aural-prosthetics version of a limited-edition Birkin bag. “This product has become a major flex for the post-70 set,” says one investor. When entertainment lawyer Allen Grubman got his—he’s buddies with an investor—he began getting calls from “very substantial” people. “They said, ‘Allen, we hear that you have these new great hearing aids,’” he says of these callers, who all wanted in. Those who finagled their way into the program include multiple Forbes 400 billionaires, a chart-topping musician, the producer of a beloved TV series, and Hollywood A-listers, both old and not-so-old. KKR private equity co-executive chair Henry Kravis raves about his Fortells, as does performer and beta tester Steve Martin.
paragraphs.zh: 在年龄相关听力损失人群中，加入 Fortell 的内测已成了一种怪异的身份象征，像限量版柏金包那样的“听力假体版”炫耀方式。“这款产品已成了 70 后后半部队的一种‘社交资本’， ”一位投资人说。娱乐律师 Allen Grubman 获得资格——他的一个投资人朋友帮了他个忙——后，开始接到“非常有分量”的人物打来的电话。他们说：“Allen，我们听说你拿到了这款很棒的新助听器。”他说这些来电者一个个都想加入。后来能挤进这个项目的人包括多位《福布斯》400强亿万富豪、一位登顶榜单的音乐人、一个备受喜爱的电视剧制片人，以及当红和不太当红的好莱坞巨星。KKR 私募股权联合执行主席亨利·克拉维斯（Henry Kravis）对他的 Fortell 赞不绝口，表演者兼内测者史蒂夫·马丁（Steve Martin）也一样。

paragraphs.en: One of Martin’s pals is actor Bob Balaban, who suffered from significant hearing damage on a movie set some years ago and was unhappy with the devices he used to mitigate the problem. Envious at Martin’s good fortune of obtaining these cutting-edge devices, he despaired that he might not get fitted himself. “God. I wish I had Steve’s hearing aids,” he told his wife. “But I think they’re movie-star hearing aids. I don’t think they’re character-actor hearing aids.” Happily, Balaban did make the cut.
paragraphs.zh: 马丁的一个朋友是演员鲍勃·巴拉班（Bob Balaban），他多年前在片场曾遭受严重听力损伤，对当时用来缓解问题的设备也不满意。看到马丁幸运地拿到了这类前沿设备，他很羡慕，担心自己也许拿不到合适的配置。“天哪，我真希望我也有 Steve 的助听器。”他对妻子说。“但我觉得它们是影帝明星戴的助听器，不是路边角色演员戴的助听器。”好在巴拉班最终也成功入选。

paragraphs.en: The people at Fortell say they hope eventually everyone who needs one can try it. The product goes on sale this month—but at only one location, with a daunting waiting list. It might be easier to procure Marty Supreme merch. The retail price will be $6,800.
paragraphs.zh: Fortell 的人表示，希望有一天需要的人都能试用这款产品。该产品将在本月上市，但目前只在一个销售点出售，并且有一条让人望而却步的等待名单。或许买一件 Marty Supreme 周边会更容易。零售价格为 6,800 美元。

paragraphs.en: Fortell’s story begins with Matt de Jonge’s grandparents. “Four times, I watched them lose their hearing, get fancy hearing aids, and just drift away,” says de Jonge, who is Fortell’s cofounder and CEO. His grandparents tumbled down a decline that is all too familiar. At first they asked people to repeat what they said, but the repetition became annoying for both sides. Sooner or later, they stopped asking, tuning out conversations that were no longer clear to them. Ultimately, as de Jonge learned, the subsequent isolation hastened a journey toward dementia. He has remorseful recollections of holiday dinners where the grandparents were ignored by family. Including him. “I had given up on them,” he says. “I feel like I have blood on my hands.”
paragraphs.zh: Fortell 的故事要从 Matt de Jonge 的祖父母开始讲起。“我看着他们四次失聪、买上高级助听器，最后逐渐退场，”Fortell 联合创始人兼首席执行官 de Jonge 说。祖父母的人生轨迹是如此熟悉的下行曲线。起初，他们会请别人重复对话内容；但反复的重复很快让双方都很烦。迟早，他们便不再反复询问，逐渐对听不清的对话选择“过滤”掉。最终，de Jonge 后来发现，这种随之而来的社交隔绝加速了他们走向痴呆的进程。他带着懊悔想起那些节日晚餐，亲人们把祖父母（包括他自己）当成空气。 “我早已放弃了他们。”他说。“我真觉得自己手上沾满了血。”

paragraphs.en: At the time de Jonge, who is now 37, was employed at the giant hedge fund Bridgewater Associates, working on its AI team. On nights and weekends he began to research what it might take to make better hearing aids. The current state is not ideal. While it’s a nearly $14 billion industry, the few key players have not managed to develop products that users love. (Some might disagree: WIRED's testers have found units worth recommending.) One 2007 study, mentioned again in 2013—around the time de Jonge started looking into this—reported that 80 percent of adults between 55 and 74 who could benefit from hearing aids don’t use them, including many who actually own them. Reasons vary from cost to comfort to the perception that the aids don’t work well enough. De Jonge realized that the key problem was in the area where hearing was most important: helping people hear conversations in social settings. Most hearing aids relied on amplifying sounds and using noise reduction, which didn’t do the trick in those scenarios.
paragraphs.zh: 当时德约翰（de Jonge）在华尔街对冲基金巨头 Bridgewater Associates 工作，担任其人工智能团队成员，现年 37 岁。夜晚和周末，他开始研究如何制造更好的助听器。当前的现状并不理想。虽然这是一个约 140 亿美元的产业，但少数几家主要厂商仍未能做出用户真正喜欢的产品。（也有人会持不同看法：WIRED 的测试者曾找到过值得推荐的机型。）2007 年的一项研究——在 2013 年再次被提及时，正好是在 de Jonge 开始研究这件事的时候——显示，55 到 74 岁、明明能从助听器中受益的成年人中有 80% 并没有使用助听器，其中也包括很多已经拥有助听器的人。原因各不相同，从价格到佩戴舒适度，再到“助听器效果不够好”的认知。de Jonge 意识到，核心问题出在听觉最关键的环节：帮助人们在社交场景中听清对话。大多数助听器只靠放大声音并进行降噪处理，在这种场景下并没有真正解决问题。

paragraphs.en: The problem was harder than it seemed. “It’s not about making sounds louder,” says de Jonge. “People with age-related hearing loss have lost some of the magic of focusing on the sounds that are important to them.” The worst situations occur in restaurants and social gatherings with lots of cross conversations. That’s known in the trade as the Cocktail Party Problem. While even people with normal hearing can struggle in those settings, those with hearing loss are lost in crescendos of conversational clatter, and costly hearing aids don’t help much. De Jonge reluctantly concluded that he had no answer for it.
paragraphs.zh: 这个问题比想象的更复杂。de Jonge 说：“这并不是把声音放得更响。” “老年性听力损失的人会失去一些重要的能力——即把注意力集中在自己关心的声音上。” 最棘手的情况发生在餐厅和人多说话交织的社交场合，这在业内被称为“鸡尾酒会问题”。即便是普通听力的人在这些环境里也常常会吃力，而听力受损者则会被各种交谈噪声淹没，昂贵的助听器也帮助不大。de Jonge 不得不承认，他自己也没有找到答案。

paragraphs.en: The exercise did motivate de Jonge to explore how technology could help solve other medical issues. He left Bridgewater and joined a startup making an AI-powered sensor called Butterfly IQ that provided “ultrasound on a chip.” He worked his way up to VP of product, and his shares in Butterfly became liquid when it went public in 2021.
paragraphs.zh: 这次经历激励了 de Jonge 去探索技术如何解决其他医疗问题。随后他离开 Bridgewater，加入一家开发名为 Butterfly IQ 的 AI 驱动传感器公司的创业公司，该设备提供“芯片级超声波”能力。他逐步晋升到产品副总裁，并且在 Butterfly 于 2021 年上市后，他持有的股份变得可以变现。

paragraphs.en: Fortell founders Matt de Jonge, Igor Lovchinsky, Andrew Casper, and Cole Morris.
paragraphs.zh: Fortell 的创始人是 Matt de Jonge、Igor Lovchinsky、Andrew Casper 和 Cole Morris。

paragraphs.en: Soon after, de Jonge and Cole Morris, a friend from his Bridgewater days and former college roommate, began pursuing an ambitious idea from Joshua Kushner, a key investor in health care startup Oscar Health who heads the VC firm Thrive Capital. Kushner was exploring whether he wanted to buy a working hospital and use it as a test bed for software that could make the operation exponentially more efficient. Soon after they started the planning, de Jonge and Morris were having second thoughts. “After four months talking to doctors about buying a hospital and ripping out all the software in it, I realized I might kill somebody,” he says. Around that time his aunt chided him about abandoning his original quest. The next generation in his family was already experiencing hearing loss. “Weren’t you going to build a better hearing aid?” she asked him.
paragraphs.zh: 不久之后，de Jonge 与科维曾一起在 Bridgewater 打交道的朋友、前大学室友科尔·莫里斯开始推进一项来自 Joshua Kushner 的宏大设想。Kushner 是医疗健康初创公司 Oscar Health 的重要投资人，亦担任风险投资公司 Thrive Capital 的负责人。他正在研究是否要收购一家正在运营的医院，并把它作为试验场，用软件使医院运营效率成倍提升。刚开始制定计划后不久，de Jonge 和莫里斯就开始动摇了。de Jonge 说：“和医生谈了四个月，讨论收购一家医院并把里面所有软件都换掉后，我意识到自己可能会害死某个人。”就在那个时候，他的姑妈批评他偏离了最初的目标。家里下一代已经开始出现听力下降了。“你不是要做一款更好的助听器吗？”她问他。

paragraphs.en: That resonated with de Jonge, whose experience with Butterfly had hinted that AI could now handle bigger challenges. He and Morris told Kushner that they wanted to drop the hospital project and instead form an AI hearing aid company. Kushner immediately said he’d fund it. “A lot of people regard AI as something you’ll use to make businesses more efficient,” he says. “But people haven't really internalized that you could use AI to make products exponentially better.”
paragraphs.zh: 这段经历与 de Jonge 的想法不谋而合，而他在 Butterfly 的经验已让他预感 AI 现在可以应对更大的挑战。他和莫里斯于是对 Kushner 说，他们想放弃医院项目，改而成立一家 AI 助听器公司。Kushner 当即表示会支持这项计划。“很多人认为 AI 是用来让企业变得更高效的工具，”他说，“但大家其实还没有真正意识到，你也可以用 AI 把产品本身提升到成倍更高的水平。”

paragraphs.en: De Jonge and Morris eventually dubbed the new company Chromatic, a name they later ditched, settling instead on Fortell. They realized that there would be two critical components in an improved approach to a hearing aid. The first would exploit the recent advances in AI for a better algorithm to selectively augment conversation. And the second would be a custom chip to process that algorithm in real time.
paragraphs.zh: de Jonge 和莫里斯最终给新公司起了个名字叫 Chromatic，但后来又放弃了这个名字，改成了 Fortell。他们意识到，改进助听器方法需要两个关键组成部分。第一是利用 AI 的最新进展，打造更好的算法来有选择性地增强对话。第二是使用一颗定制芯片，在实时环境下处理该算法。

paragraphs.en: The first requirement became the province of Igor Lovchinsky, who had been Butterfly’s AI wizard. He’d come to the field late in life; up until his mid-twenties he’d been a Juilliard-trained concert pianist but left the field when he became enamored with science. Lovchinsky felt that the AI claims made by some other hearing aid companies were overblown; they were simply tweaking the amplification, he says, or aiming the microphones in a different direction.
paragraphs.zh: 第一个任务便落到了伊戈尔·洛温金斯基（Igor Lovchinsky）身上，他是 Butterfly 的 AI 大拿。他是后来才转入这个领域的；直到二十多岁中期，他都是受茱莉亚德音乐学院（Juilliard）训练的音乐会钢琴家，但当他沉迷于科学后便离开了这个行业。Lovchinsky 认为，其他一些助听器公司宣称的 AI 功能被过度夸大了；他表示，他们只是简单调整了放大参数，或者把麦克风朝向改了方向。

paragraphs.en: Demonstration video, provided by Fortell, of its hearing aid technology in action.
paragraphs.zh: Fortell 提供的其助听器技术实战演示视频。

paragraphs.en: “What became clear is that what was needed is source separation,” he says. “Take an audio wave that contains both things you want to hear and things you don't want to hear, and separate them into just speech and just noise.” Even in 2021, it wasn’t clear that this was possible. “We all have this incredible neural network in our heads honed by billions of years of evolution to recognize speech,” he says. “If you do the source separation with the slightest deviation from full naturalness, your brain will immediately hear it.”
paragraphs.zh: “后来就更清楚了，我们真正需要的是声音源分离，”他说。“把一段既有你想听的内容、也有你不想听的内容的音频波形，分成只有语音和只有噪音两部分。”即使在 2021 年，也没人明确这是否可行。“我们每个人都在大脑里拥有一个经过数十亿年进化磨炼出来的神经网络，用于识别语音，”他说。“如果做源分离时哪怕有一点偏离自然语音的地方，你的大脑都会立刻听出来。”

paragraphs.en: As the company’s cofounder and chief scientific officer, Lovchinsky and his team set about using cutting-edge AI to identify the aural fingerprints of the voices directed to the wearer, clean them up, and pass them on as if delivered in a quieter setting.
paragraphs.zh: 作为公司联合创始人兼首席科学官，Lovchinsky 和他的团队开始用最前沿的 AI 技术识别指向佩戴者的声音“指纹”，将其清晰化后再转交给佩戴者，就像在更安静的环境里听到的一样。

paragraphs.en: A look at the AI-powered device.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一款采用 AI 技术驱动的设备外观。

paragraphs.en: Having the right algorithms wouldn’t be worth much if you didn’t have a properly engineered chip to run them. To lead its silicon team, Fortell tapped as CTO Andrew Casper, another Butterfly alum who was a lead engineer on a Google team making AI chips. Casper also wasn’t sure that his task could be accomplished. “Your ear is very sensitive to latency,” he says, noting that if the altered sounds weren’t processed in 10 milliseconds—a hundredth of a second—it would throw users into a hellish uncanny valley. “We didn’t know if it could be done in that amount of time with a high enough fidelity so you aren’t going to notice distortions.” Only then, he says, could the company move to the final challenge: “Can we even put this thing into your ear?”
paragraphs.zh: 如果没有一颗工程上真正可行的芯片，再优秀的算法也不值一提。为了带领其硅团队，Fortell 聘请了首席技术官 Andrew Casper，他也是 Butterfly 的校友，曾是谷歌一个研发 AI 芯片团队的首席工程师。Casper 也不确定这项任务是否真的能完成。“你的耳朵对延迟极其敏感，”他说，“如果处理后声像没有在 10 毫秒——也就是百分之一秒——内完成，就会把用户推入一种痛苦且诡异的体验谷。” “我们当时也不知道能否在这么短的时间内做到足够高的保真度，让你察觉不到失真。” 只有在这步做到之后，他说，公司才可能进入最终挑战：“我们能不能真的把这个东西装进你的耳朵里？”

paragraphs.en: It was going to take years before the startup got those things right and could even begin to test on humans. Fortunately, the $9 million initial stake, the majority of which came from Kushner, provided a long runway. “For the first few years of the company there was no hearing aid in sight,” says de Jonge. “We needed to build for ourselves to see if the science problems could be solved.”
paragraphs.zh: 距离把这些问题解决、并开始对真人做测试，创业公司还要再花好几年时间。幸运的是，首轮 900 万美元的投资——其中大部分来自 Kushner——为公司提供了充足的 runway。德容·德容说：“公司成立的最初几年，根本看不到任何成品助听器。” “因为我们需要先为自己打造一套系统，看看这些科学问题是否能被真正解决。”

paragraphs.en: By 2023, Lovchinsky and Casper had made significant progress on their respective missions. Lovchinsky’s team realized that separating out the voices required creating a proprietary version of what is known in the industry as Spatial AI, involving a 3D understanding of the real world. (Confusingly, they also use the nonproprietary technology, spatial AI, in their product.) “It gleans perspectives from multiple microphones and can infer the same way that healthy people can, from both ears,” he says. His team also found a way to train their AI models with huge amounts of synthetic data that emulated all sorts of conditions. “It’s specifically useful in the most challenging environments,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 到 2023 年，Lovchinsky 和 Casper 在各自任务上都已取得显著进展。Lovchinsky 团队意识到，要把声音分离出来，就必须开发出业界所称的 Spatial AI 的专有版本，即对真实世界的三维感知。（令人有点困惑的是，他们在产品里也在使用同名的非专有技术，叫 spatial AI。）“它从多个麦克风中提取听觉线索，能够像正常人一样通过双耳进行推断，”他表示。该团队还找到了一种方法，用海量合成数据训练 AI 模型，这些数据模拟了各种不同环境。“这在最具挑战性的环境下尤其有用，”他补充道。

paragraphs.en: More rounds of funding followed, with $150 million invested so far. The B round was co-led by Antonio Gracias, known for his involvement in Tesla and the so-called Department of Government Efficiency. (He’s using that Tesla experience to provide guidance on scaling Fortell.) De Jonge gave him the street demo; the product is of special interest to Gracias because Gracias’ sister is a dentist who has hearing loss because of the constant drill noise. “Even with construction behind me, I could hear Matt clearly,” he says, “and [it] literally brought me to tears.” Gracia’s sister is now in the beta test.
paragraphs.zh: 公司随后又完成了更多轮融资，目前累计投入金额达到 1.5 亿美元。B 轮由 Antonio Gracias 共同领投，他因参与特斯拉以及所谓“美国政府效率部”而广为人知。（他正是凭借在特斯拉积累的经验，为 Fortell 提供了扩张方面的指导。）de Jonge 还给他做了一个街头演示；该产品对 Gracias 尤其有吸引力，因为 Gracias 的姐姐是一名牙医，由于长期的钻机噪音出现了听力损失。“即使我身后有施工在进行，我也能清楚听到 Matt 的声音，”他说，“这确实让我热泪盈眶。” Gracia 的姐姐现在已经进入了内测。

paragraphs.en: Another early investor was Founders Fund partner Trae Stephens. While his general hearing is pretty good, Stephens, 42, had been noticing problems in noisy rooms. He was blown away with a demonstration. “It was honestly the best hardware demo I've seen in my 11 and a half years at Founder’s Fund,” he says. “Oh, man, if I could wear these to a loud coffee shop and have a meeting, it would change my life.”
paragraphs.zh: 另一位早期投资人是Founders Fund的合伙人Trae Stephens。虽然他的整体听力相当不错，但42岁的Stephens在嘈杂的房间里一直感到一些听力问题。一次演示让他非常震撼。“说实话，这是我在Founders Fund 11年半里见过的最棒硬件演示。”他表示：“天啊，如果我能在嘈杂的咖啡店里戴着它开会，那会改变我的生活。”

paragraphs.en: Meryl Hartzband and audiologist Kathleen Wallace at Fortell.
paragraphs.zh: Fortell的Meryl Hartzband与听力学家Kathleen Wallace。

paragraphs.en: Those early demos came before the chip was ready. The first testers wore an awkward rig with headphones attached to a laptop that processed the sounds. “We took people to coffee shops and restaurants and apologized to the waitstaff, explaining we were testing some hearing aid technology,” says Morris. “Mostly they let us do our thing.”
paragraphs.zh: 那些早期演示是在芯片尚未就绪之前进行的。最早的测试者戴着一套笨重的设备，耳机连接着一台处理声音的笔记本电脑。“我们把人带到咖啡店和餐厅，并向服务人员道歉，解释我们正在测试一项助听器技术。”Morris说。“大多数时候，他们都允许我们照常进行测试。”

paragraphs.en: De Jonge had secretly made a list of potential high-profile beta testers. He had no idea if they had hearing difficulties, just that they had reached a certain age when many if not most people have trouble. One of those early users was KKR’s Kravis, an early Fortell investor who had been trying different hearing aids for years. He was so impressed that he wanted to increase his stake, which ultimately became $6.2 million. Kravis also offered to connect the team with some high-profile beta testers. So he sent his friend Steve Martin, a fellow art collector, to SoHo.
paragraphs.zh: De Jonge暗中列出了一份潜在的高知名度测试者名单。他不知道这些人是否有听力障碍，只知道他们已经到了很多人甚至多数人都会出现听力问题的年龄。早期用户之一是KKR的Kravis，一位早期Fortell投资者，几年来一直在尝试不同的助听器。他被产品深深打动，进而想要增加投资份额，最终这笔投资升至620万美元。Kravis还主动提议帮助团队对接一些高知名度测试者。于是他把他的朋友、也是艺术收藏家、斯蒂夫·马丁送到了SoHo。

paragraphs.en: “I’ve tried different brands of hearing aids, and they're good, but they're not this good,” says Martin in a Zoom interview. He visited the team in Soho, did the street test, and was delighted when he tried it with his wife and daughter at their favorite restaurant, with de Jonge sitting with the laptop several tables away. But the clincher for Martin was a cocktail party.
paragraphs.zh: “我试过不同牌子的助听器，它们都不错，但没有这款好。”马丁在一次 Zoom 访谈中说。他曾去 Soho 的团队办公室拜访，进行了“街测”，并且在和妻子、女儿一起去他们最喜欢的餐厅试用时非常高兴，德·容（de Jonge）则坐在隔了几桌、抱着笔记本电脑。可对马丁来说，最关键的时刻是在一次鸡尾酒会。

paragraphs.en: “I was here in our building, and I was at a party upstairs, and I had my old hearing aids in,” he says. “I’m sitting talking to four people, and I realized I can't understand any of them, and I go, wait, I have these new hearing aids. I went downstairs, put them in, came back, and I could hear everyone.” Now he wears them all the time, and even made a joke about hearing aids on Saturday Night Live’s 50th anniversary special. “I don't really think about the way it used to be,” he says. “I used to dread going to a restaurant, and now I don't.” His friend Balaban, once he got into the beta test, is similarly smitten. “This is a significant improvement over the absurdly pricey devices I’d been using,” Balaban says.
paragraphs.zh: “我那天在大楼里，楼上有个聚会，我戴着我的旧助听器。”他说，“我正和四个人聊天，突然意识到一句都听不懂，于是想到，等等，我还有这些新助听器。”我下去把它们戴上，再回来后，立刻就能听见每个人说话了。”现在他几乎全天佩戴它，甚至在《周六夜现场》50 周年特别节目里还开玩笑提起助听器。“我真的不再像以前那样‘想起’这件事了，”他说。“以前我一想到去餐厅就很恐慌，现在不会了。”同样，巴拉班（Balaban）在参加测试后也深深着迷。“这比我以前用的那种离谱贵的设备有了明显提升。”巴拉班说。

paragraphs.en: Other machers aren’t public, but de Jonge assures me they are mostly names invoked in boldface type. Since there are only a few dozen beta units, this means that some powerful people have been shuttled to a waiting list. Balaban’s wife, Lynn Grossman, recounts attending a Labor Day dinner with over 100 people, generally of a certain age, in a private room in a restaurant, thinking that her husband and another guy—a famous CEO in the fashion world—were the only ones who could hear, because of Fortell. “After, I think Bob got 12 or 14 emails saying, ‘How do I get those hearing aids?’”
paragraphs.zh: 其他试戴者并未公开露面，但德·容向我保证，他们大多只是在粗体字里出现名字。由于早期测试机只有几十台，因此可以说明一些有影响力的人已经被安排进了候补名单。巴拉班的妻子林·格罗斯曼（Lynn Grossman）回忆，他们曾在一家餐厅包下私人包厢，参加一场有一百多人、以特定年龄层为主的劳动节晚宴，大家都以为她丈夫和另一位“一个在时装界非常有名的 CEO”是唯一能听清的人，因为他们用了 Fortell。“后来我想，鲍勃大概收到了 12 封或 14 封邮件，邮件内容是：‘这个助听器怎么拿？’”

paragraphs.en: Now that the product is launched, Fortell will sell hearing aids in a single clinic on Manhattan’s Park Avenue. It’s decked out like a posh lounge, with the devices on display in a tasteful presentation that’s straight out of the Apple retail playbook. Hanging on the wall is a silicon wafer with the circuitry of the custom chips. In the early stages, his staff of four audiologists will serve only a couple of dozen customers a week, to make sure everything goes smoothly. In any case, while ramping up production, the supply will be limited.
paragraphs.zh: 产品正式上市后，Fortell 将在曼哈顿公园大道的一家门诊部销售助听器。该门诊部布置得像一家高档休闲会所，展陈则采用了非常像苹果零售店的“苹果式”陈列方式，设备被摆放得很有格调。墙上挂着一片硅片，展示着其定制芯片的电路。产品早期阶段，四名听力学专家的团队每周只接待几十名顾客，以确保一切顺利。无论如何，在产能爬坡期间，供应都会是紧张而有限的。

paragraphs.en: The chips used inside the hearing aids, on display in the lobby of Fortell.
paragraphs.zh: 展厅大厅里展示的，正是 Fortell 助听器内置的芯片。

paragraphs.en: This is great for Fortell, but it seems de Jonge’s initial impulse to usher everyone’s grandparents into the land of the hearing is in danger of being limited to the one percent, which doesn’t exactly qualify him for a Salk medal. When I ask de Jonge how his invention can scale to change life for the masses, his replies, whether due to secrecy on future plans or just not having a good answer, seem hand-wavy. In his defense, Fortell has resisted the temptation to jack up the traditional price of premium hearing aids—the $6,800 is actually a bit less than some other medically prescribed hearing aids. (As with other high-end hearing aids, the price is part of a package that includes fitting and support from professional audiologists.) Still, even that defensible price tag limits adoption; it’s a sad fact that some Medicare and many health insurance plans do not cover hearing aids, a policy that dooms millions to an aural bardo of conversational exclusion, isolating them from loved ones and hastening dementia.
paragraphs.zh: 这对 Fortell 来说是件好事，但德·容格（de Jonge）最初将每个人的祖父母都带入“听见世界”之境的冲动似乎正面临被限制在最富有的那一个百分点之内的危险，这也不足以让他配得上像萨尔克奖那样的荣誉。当我询问德·容格，他的发明如何扩展到普通大众并改变他们的生活时，他的回答——无论是出于对未来计划保密，还是他真的没有完整答案——都显得有些含糊。就他而言，Fortell 已经抵制了提高高端助听器传统定价的诱惑：其 6,800 美元定价实际上比一些其他处方助听器还低一点。（和其他高端助听器一样，这个价格包含了由专业听力师进行配戴、调音和后续支持的整体服务。）尽管如此，即使是这个相对站得住脚的价格，也限制了普及；令人遗憾的是，许多 Medicare 项目和众多健康保险计划并不报销助听器，这一政策让数百万人陷入“听觉性生存边缘”：他们在对话中被排斥，与亲友逐渐疏离，并加速了痴呆风险。

paragraphs.en: It’s unclear whether Fortell technology might find its way into the less expensive over-the-counter hearing aids available today, which became possible via a Biden-era shift in regulation. These include Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 devices and entries from other consumer electronics brands, which are generally known to help those with hearing loss but not as much as high-end devices that are paired with professional support. The Fortell proposition requires careful testing and tuning, continuing for some time as wearers get used to the devices. In any case, that white-glove approach will consume Fortell’s efforts for the next year and more. Expansion will come by opening clinics in a few select cities, and only later will Fortell consider scaling to allow others to sell the technology.
paragraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚 Fortell 的技术是否可能进入如今更便宜的“场外销售”助听器市场——这种市场得益于拜登任期内监管规则调整而出现。此类产品包括苹果的 AirPods Pro 2 以及其他消费电子品牌的产品，通常只能在一定程度上帮助有听力损失的人，远不及配合专业人员支持的高端设备来得强大。Fortell 的方案需要持续进行严谨的测试与调校，并在使用者逐步适应设备的过程中持续调整。无论如何，这种“白手套式”服务都将消耗 Fortell 未来一年乃至更长时间的主要精力。公司将先在少数重点城市开设诊所，只有在之后，Fortell 才会考虑扩大规模，让更多机构参与销售其技术。

paragraphs.en: By now you’re probably wondering … who is paying this WIRED reporter to pump up the prospects of a new startup? Is it really that good? It’s hard to measure hearing quality, but Fortell has set out to prove scientifically that it has a better solution to hearing loss. It contracted researchers in NYU Langone’s audiology and neuroscience departments to consult on a blind experiment comparing Fortell with the leading AI-powered hearing aid competitor, a Swiss company called Phonak, whose devices retail for $4,000 and is considered the gold standard in AI hearing products. (In the study, Phonak isn’t mentioned by name and is identified only as the control hearing aid group.)
paragraphs.zh: 到这里，你可能已经在想：是谁在给这位《WIRED》记者付钱，让他大肆吹捧这家新创公司？它到底有那么好吗？听力质量并不容易量化，但 Fortell 已经着手以科学方式证明自己有更好的听力损失解决方案。该公司聘请了纽约大学朗格奈医学院听觉学与神经科学部门的研究人员，以双盲实验形式，与市场领先的 AI 助听器竞争者——一家名为 Phonak 的瑞士公司——进行对比。Phonak 的设备零售价为 4,000 美元，被视为 AI 助听产品的金标准。（在研究中，Phonak 没有被点名，而是仅以“对照组助听器”身份出现。）

paragraphs.en: The test matched performance in environments where noise was coming at random intervals from three directions—kind of an emulation of the Cocktail Party Problem. “This is a configuration that's particularly good to show the advantages of this aid, because what it does is actually extracting the various signals and getting rid of some of them,” says Mario Svirsky, the Noel L. Cohen Professor of Hearing Science at NYU School of Medicine, who consulted in the study (and was paid for his time).
paragraphs.zh: 该测试在噪音从三个方向随机间歇出现的环境中匹配了表现——这在某种程度上模拟了“鸡尾酒会问题”。纽约大学医学院听觉科学诺埃尔·L·科恩教授、该研究的顾问兼受偿咨询者 Mario Svirsky 说：“这是一个特别能体现这款助听器优势的设置，因为它实际上是在提取各种信号并去掉其中的一部分。”

paragraphs.en: Svirsky says the test and its goals were set out in advance. If it showed that Fortell notched a 4-decibel increase over its rival in boosting the desired signal, it would be a home run. But when they ran the study, the difference reported between the two devices was 9.2 dB in Fortell’s favor. “The results were overwhelming,” he says. “I’ve never seen such a categorical result in my career.” In one chart, the line representing the hearing improvement from Fortell virtually towered over the Phonak line. The study concluded, “In the most challenging multi-talker environment participants had 18.9X higher odds of understanding speech versus the top AI hearing aids on the market today.”
paragraphs.zh: Svirsky 表示，测试及其目标在前期已先行设定。如果结果显示 Fortell 在增强目标信号方面相比竞争产品提高了4分贝，那将是非常出色的成绩；但当他们进行研究后，报告显示两款设备之间的差异达到了9.2分贝，且有利于 Fortell。“结果十分压倒性。”他说，“我职业生涯中从未见过如此明确的结果。”在一张图表中，Fortell 带来的听力改善曲线几乎彻底高于 Phonak 的曲线。该研究得出结论：“在最具挑战性的多说话人环境下，参与者理解语音的机会是当今市场上顶级 AI 助听器的18.9倍。”

paragraphs.en: The Fortell hearing aid in Meryl Hartzband’s ear.
paragraphs.zh: Meryl Hartzband 耳中的 Fortell 助听器。

paragraphs.en: Naturally, I sought comment from Phonak about those results. Michael Preuss, the lead audiologist for Phonak’s AI platform, has been wearing hearing aids since he was 3 years old. Phonak, he says, has been in the business for 75 years and has been working with AI in its products for the last quarter century, and for the last seven years has pursued the idea of producing an AI chip—just like Fortell. Phonak, too, has spent years developing and testing its AI system, which rolled out last year to what the company describes as acclaim and adoption. When I tell Preuss about how some startup he never heard of trounced his product in a head-to-head test, he seems unruffled. “We have seen in the past that there is no industry standard in how you set up these studies and how you do these kinds of measurements,” he says. “You can design studies to enhance your own performance.” To be sure, Fortell did set up conditions that played to its strengths. But Svirsky says that those conditions were the ones that matter to hearing aid wearers. Also, unlike almost all studies performed by hearing aid companies, Fortell has submitted its work for publication in a peer-reviewed journal.
paragraphs.zh: 我自然向 Phonak 申请了对这些结果的回应。Phonak AI 平台的首席听力专家 Michael Preuss 从3岁起就开始佩戴助听器。Preuss 说，Phonak 从事该行业已有75年，并在近25年一直在其产品中使用 AI，过去7年一直在推进研发一颗 AI 芯片——与 Fortell 的做法类似。Phonak 也已历经多年开发和测试其 AI 系统，该系统去年推出后，受到了公司所说的“赞誉和采用”。当我告诉 Preuss，一家他从未听说过的初创公司在面对面测试中远胜于他的产品时，他看起来并不惊慌。“我们过去看到过，业内并没有统一标准来规定如何设定这些研究以及如何进行这类测量。”他说，“你可以把研究设计成有利于自己成绩的样子。”确实，Fortell 确实设置了更能发挥其优势的测试条件。但 Svirsky 说，这些条件正是助听器使用者真正关心的场景。另外，与几乎所有助听器公司所做的研究不同，Fortell 已将其成果提交到同行评审期刊发表。

paragraphs.en: Still, because of what seemed to be cherry-picked conditions—and the study’s funding coming from the company itself—skeptical observers might not see the test as a slam dunk. And who knows, perhaps all those delighted Fortell beta users who refuse to return the units after their test period are experiencing kind of an insidery placebo effect. It also could be that the extra attention given to beta testers encouraged users to be more diligent about visiting the audiologists multiple times for perfect tuning. So I decided to test the Fortell aids for myself.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管看起来像是经过精心挑选的测试条件——再加上研究经费来自公司本身——怀疑者可能并不认为这项测试是十拿九稳的结论。况且，那些在试用期结束后都不肯把设备归还的、兴奋的 Fortell 体验用户，或许只是出现了一种隐蔽的安慰剂效应。同样，也可能是因为给测试者额外的关注，让他们更勤于多次去听力师处反复调音。因此我决定亲自测试 Fortell 的助听器。

paragraphs.en: I have had a hearing loss problem for years. I blame it on an ear-shattering 1969 Who concert (worth it), but my doctor says it’s more likely a combination of childhood ear infections and, well, getting old. I am an unsatisfied customer of a high-end hearing aid. So I was eager to test Fortell’s wares, starting with the street demo. No, I did not burst out in tears. But I was cautiously impressed. Once I got fitted and fine-tuned—this involved a hearing test and several sessions with a Fortell audiologist—I began using the aids daily. The comparison with my old devices wasn’t head-to-head, but I sensed noticeable improvement.
paragraphs.zh: 我多年来一直有听力问题。我把它归咎于一次撼人耳膜的 1969 年 Who 演唱会（值得）。但我的医生说更可能是童年时反复耳部感染，再加上年龄增长的结果。我一直是高端助听器的一个不满意用户。因此我很想从街头演示开始测试 Fortell 的产品。并不是说我当场就落泪了，但我确实谨慎地被打动。等我完成配戴与微调——包括一次听力测试和几次与 Fortell 听力师会面——后，我开始每天使用这些助听器。与我旧设备的比较并非完全同场对比，但我感觉到明显的改善。

paragraphs.en: Fortell is no miracle: In really noisy conditions, things are still hopeless. But to be fair, even people with perfect hearing are usually shouting at each other in those situations. (Who told restaurants that Led Zeppelin–level noise was the perfect accompaniment to dining?) Absent, say, a DJ and a wall of speakers, Fortell really did crack the Cocktail Party Problem. Compared to the expensive hearing aids I was using, I could follow more conversations during restaurant meals. I found myself comfortable with using them all day, whereas I couldn’t wait to take off the ones I had paid $8,000 for. (Apologies to Phonak—I haven’t tried those.) The biggest test was how well I could hear my wife, whose dulcet voice is sometimes the hardest one for me to make out. Using these new devices, I am less likely to respond to her trenchant observations with the word “What?”
paragraphs.zh: Fortell 并非奇迹：在真正嘈杂的环境里，听感依然很糟。但公平地说，即使听力完好的人大多也会在这种情况下对着对方大声说话。（谁规定餐厅里要配有 Led Zeppelin 级别的噪音才能用餐？）只要没有 DJ 和一面音箱墙，Fortell 真的把“鸡尾酒会问题”搞定了。和我正在用的那种昂贵助听器相比，我在餐厅用餐时能跟上更多对话。以前戴着那副我花了 8000 美元买的设备时，我总想赶紧摘掉；而现在我却能舒适地整天戴着。〔向 Phonak 道歉——我没有试过他们的产品。〕最重要的测试，是我能否听清妻子说的话。她那带有醇厚温柔感的嗓音，有时反而是我最难听懂的。用了这些新设备后，我不太会对她那锐利的提醒只是回一句“什么？”

paragraphs.en: Bottom line: Now that Fortell is open for business, I’m going to ditch my present units and drop almost seven grand to buy a pair. If I can get on the list.
paragraphs.zh: 结论是：既然 Fortell 已经正式开门营业，我打算淘汰现在的设备，掏出将近七千美元买一副。如果我能排上名单的话。

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article in the comments below. Alternatively, submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎在下方留言区告诉我们你对这篇文章的看法。或者，也可以向编辑部投稿，电邮至 [email protected]。
